methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
44.01 is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $37M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 private peers in the Climate & Energy sector—including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen—alongside one public comparable (LNZA). However, the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints meeting minimum density thresholds, rendering traditional peer-median analysis inapplicable.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology provides the primary valuation signal. Applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to later-stage progression to the $37M total raised baseline yields $74M. This reflects the incremental value creation expected between Series A and subsequent funding rounds, absent company-specific operational or market data that would warrant upward or downward adjustment.

The triangulated valuation range spans $63M (low) to $85M (high), centered on $74M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than methodological triangulation. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier absent granular burn rate, runway, and technology-risk data; the eventual availability of closer peer comparables as the sector matures; and sector-specific investor appetite for Climate & Energy infrastructure at this stage. Material valuation revision would follow disclosure of revenue, capital efficiency metrics, or narrowed technical risk.
